reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully formalizes the informal statement. `IsPrimitiveRoot (a : ZMod p) (p-1)` in Mathlib (requiring `a^(p-1) = 1` and `(p-1) ∣ l` whenever `a^l = 1`) is equivalent to "a has multiplicative order p-1 mod p", i.e., a primitive root modulo p. The hypotheses `Odd p` and `p.Prime` match "odd prime". The RHS `∀ q, q ∣ (p-1) → q.Prime → ¬(a^((p-1)/q) = 1)` precisely encodes "for all prime divisors q of p-1, a^((p-1)/q) ≢ 1 (mod p)". Using `a : (ZMod p)ˣ` (a unit) surfaces the implicit premise that a is coprime to p — without it the iff would literally fail (for a ≡ 0 mod p, the LHS is false but the RHS holds vacuously), so this is exactly the "implicit premise needed for truth" case and counts as faithful.
